title: Rajasthan reels under intense heatwave, temperatures may touch 47 degrees - Metro Vaartha
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Rajasthan
full_text:
Jaipur | Heatwave conditions intensified across Rajasthan with temperatures in western parts hovering between 45 and 46 degrees Celsius and Jaisalmer recording the highest maximum temperature of 45.9 degrees Celsius in the past 24 hours, the Meteorological Department said.
The Meteorological Centre in Jaipur has issued a heatwave alert, warning that weather conditions are likely to remain dry across most parts of the state for the next three to four days. Dust-laden winds with speeds of 20-30 kmph are also expected, along with a further rise of 2-3 degrees Celsius in temperatures.
The department said that on May 26 and 27, some parts of western Rajasthan may witness maximum temperatures reaching 46-47 degrees Celsius, with isolated places likely to experience severe heatwave conditions.
However, a fresh western disturbance is expected to bring some relief from May 28-29, with possibilities of strong thunderstorms and winds reaching 50-60 kmph, and light rainfall in parts of the state. This is likely to lower maximum temperatures by 2-3 degrees Celsius.
Weather officials added that thunderstorm and rain activity may continue into the first week of June, providing intermittent respite from the prevailing heatwave conditions.
